title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL sink should pass integer connection timeouts to the driver
summary: The Oracle MySQL sink accepts a positive numeric connection timeout, but Oracle MySQL Connector/Python 9.7.0 can reject a floating-point timeout before the connection pool is created.
observed: During the Oracle MySQL sink container e2e run for issue #101, the driver raised a TypeError while opening the connection pool because the timeout value was passed as a float.
expected: The Oracle MySQL sink should normalize the validated timeout to the integer seconds shape expected by the driver before creating the connection pool.
reproduction: Run the Oracle MySQL sink container e2e harness with the Oracle MySQL optional dependency installed and the default sink configuration.
failing_test: Add a focused unit test that builds an Oracle MySQL sink with a fractional timeout and asserts the generated pool options pass an integer timeout to the driver.
impact: The bug blocks the new Oracle MySQL sink from completing its local container-backed e2e certification even though connection settings and credentials are otherwise valid.
delivery_semantics: The failure happens during sink startup before any NATS messages are fetched. It does not create early ACKs, duplicate writes, DLQ writes, or partial Oracle MySQL commits.
security: The fix should not log host details, credentials, or database names. Timeout normalization is a local type-safety change and keeps the existing redaction and least-privilege guidance intact.
acceptance: The Oracle MySQL sink passes an integer connection timeout to Oracle MySQL Connector/Python.
Fractional positive timeout configuration remains accepted but is normalized before driver use.
A focused regression test proves the timeout option shape.
The Oracle MySQL container e2e harness reaches the database write path instead of failing at pool creation.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL sink unit tests, the public API and sink certification subsets, and the Oracle MySQL sink container e2e harness.
documentation: No public configuration syntax changes are required. Update the changelog and test report with the regression and e2e evidence.
closeout: Mark completed after the regression test and Oracle MySQL e2e test pass on the issue branch and sanitized evidence is posted.
